page: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-8-lssd is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. For local storage, it includes 804 GB of NVMe SSD capacity distributed across two local SSDs.

Benchmark results place this instance consistently in the average range for most operations. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1702.0 and a multi-core score of 6546.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 544.39 GB/sec for cached small block reads, dropping to 55.2 GB/sec for uncached large block reads. The instance demonstrates stable performance in database operations, recording 3909.21 on PassMark database tests and handling 8.89 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks.

While the instance delivers average performance across standard workloads like static web serving (13.23 GB/sec) and software compilation, certain multi-threaded tasks show limitations. Specifically, bzip3 multi-threaded decompression is weak, falling into the bottom 25% of benchmarks. Similarly, while prompt processing for a 7B LLM model reaches 51.34 tokens/sec, text generation for the same model is weak at 7.69 tokens/sec. Qualitatively, the inclusion of high-speed local NVMe SSDs alongside dedicated compute resources offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads requiring low-latency local storage without the overhead of external disk attachments.

This instance is configured for workloads such as relational databases, caching layers like Redis, software development environments, and general-purpose web serving. The presence of Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) also supports entry-level vector and matrix math operations, though heavy LLM text generation is constrained by the hardware profile.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-8-lssd is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 804 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It delivers average performance across most benchmarks, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 6546.0 and 8.89 million operations per second on Redis SET tests. The instance features Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Cryptography acceleration. However, it exhibits weaker performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and 7B LLM text generation. Qualitatively, the bundled high-speed local storage provides a resource-dense configuration for data-intensive applications. This server is suitable for database operations, caching layers, and general-purpose web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-8-lssd is an 8 vCPU, 32 GB RAM general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon processor and 804 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-8-lssd features 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 804 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for balanced general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and integrated high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor at 2.7 GHz
32.0 GB of RAM paired with 804 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Average performance in database operations and Redis SET benchmarks
Hardware acceleration via Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX)
Weaker performance in multi-threaded decompression and 7B LLM text generation
Suitable for relational databases, caching layers, and web serving
